new_direction: numerical-embeddings: canonical per-feature numerical embeddings (PLE/Periodic/PLR)
query: On Embeddings for Numerical Features in Tabular Deep Learning arXiv:2203.05556
why_new: Defines the canonical numerical-feature tokenizer (PLE/Periodic/PLR) for the continuous half of a 70+-mixed-feature tokenizer; surfaces per-feature param blowup and target-aware bin leakage.
next_followups: wire PLR into FT-Transformer numerical branch
control PLEt out-of-fold bin leakage
stress-test at 70+ numerical features
compare PLR vs AutoDis
result_status_detail: Deep-read via arXiv abstract + full PDF text extraction; extracted PLE/Periodic/PLR formulas, bin-construction (quantile + C4.5 tree), full ablation/result tables, param-blowup costs, preprocessing robustness, GBDT non-transfer.

new_direction: tabular-transformers: canonical per-feature Feature Tokenizer (FT-Transformer)
query: Revisiting Deep Learning Models for Tabular Data FT-Transformer feature tokenizer
why_new: Foundational anchor for the tabular-transformers route; defines the unified per-feature mixed-type token (scalar*vector+bias for numerical, lookup+bias for categorical) that later tokenizers extend.
next_followups: deep-read On Embeddings for Numerical Features (PLR/periodic) as the numerical-branch upgrade
deep-read TabTransformer/SAINT to contrast token granularity and contextual categorical embeddings
examine efficient-attention tabular variants for the O(k^2) scaling limit

new_direction: categorical-high-cardinality foundational anchor
query: Entity Embeddings of Categorical Variables arXiv:1604.06737
why_new: Seminal pre-2018 primitive for learned per-field categorical embeddings; ancestor of all deep tabular tokenizers; not previously recorded.
next_followups: TabTransformer
FT-Transformer
target/CatBoost encoding comparison
discretize-then-embed numerics

new_direction: temporal-feature-tokenization: patch tokenizer + masked-patch pretraining (PatchTST)
query: PatchTST A Time Series is Worth 64 Words long-term forecasting transformers
why_new: First coverage of the temporal route: supplies the canonical time-varying tokenizer (subseries patching) + a masked self-supervised pretext for the topic's time-varying half. Channel-independence is a deliberate scope mismatch (drops cross-feature interaction).
next_followups: fuse patch tokens with static FT-Transformer tokens
replace linear patch projection with PLR/periodic
iTransformer/TFT for static-covariate fusion
isolate RevIN vs patching contribution

new_direction: feature-interaction-selection: field-shared self-attention interacting layer (AutoInt)
query: AutoInt automatic feature interaction self-attentive neural networks CTR
why_new: First coverage of the feature-interaction route: explicit multi-head self-attention interacting layer whose params are O(1) in feature count M (compute O(M^2)). Directly attacks the 70+-feature interaction problem; reuse the interaction layer, not its rank-1 numerical embedding.
next_followups: bolt interacting layer onto FT-Transformer/PLR tokens
DCN-V2/xDeepFM cheaper interaction
efficient/sparse attention for O(M^2) wall
feature gating before interaction

new_direction: tabular-transformers / benchmark-critique: WHY deep tabular lags (Grinsztajn 2022)
query: Why do tree-based models still outperform deep learning on tabular data 2207.08815
why_new: Challenge/motivation anchor: 45-dataset fixed-budget benchmark + inductive-bias diagnosis (rotation invariance, irregular targets, uninformative-feature fragility) that explains WHY per-feature embeddings help and why feature count matters. Explicitly excludes high-cardinality / missing / temporal — the topic's hardest cases.
next_followups: large-data regime where the gap narrows (TabPFN v2)
does rotation-invariance failure persist with high-card + temporal channels
use LeoGrin/tabular-benchmark as fixed-budget yardstick

new_direction: tabular-foundation-models: per-cell two-way-attention PFN tokenization
query: TabPFN v2 Nature 2025 architecture per-cell two-way attention synthetic SCM pretraining benchmarks vs CatBoost/XGBoost/AutoGluon limits
source: nature.com (auth-gated landing) + github.com/PriorLabs/TabPFN README + Wikipedia + INWT replication blog + web search
why_new: First tabular-foundation-model in the corpus; introduces per-cell tokenization + alternating feature/sample attention + prior-data-fitted synthetic-SCM pretraining, contrasting with the per-feature tokenizers (FT-Transformer, AutoInt) already recorded.
next_followups: read TabPFN-2.5 and TabPFN-3 (2026) for wide-table scaling
compare CARTE / XTab / TabICL
verify exact Nature benchmark tables once a non-gated PDF is available

new_direction: temporal-static-fusion via TFT Variable Selection Networks
query: Temporal Fusion Transformer interpretable multi-horizon forecasting arXiv 1912.09363
why_new: First record covering the temporal-static-fusion concept layer with a learned per-feature soft-selection tokenizer (VSN) over mixed numeric+categorical+static+temporal inputs — complements PatchTST (which lacks any static/categorical pathway).
next_followups: compare VSN gating vs AutoInt/DCN explicit interaction at scale
test VSN + PLR numerical embeddings
verify TFT param growth at 70+ features

query: DCN V2 Improved Deep & Cross Network web-scale learning to rank
why_new: Adds the field-count-scalable explicit-interaction operator (full-matrix Hadamard cross + low-rank MoE) as the primary alternative to AutoInt on the feature-interaction-selection route.
next_followups: Head-to-head DCN-V2 cross vs AutoInt attention on same 70-feature tokenizer at matched params measuring accuracy and wall-clock
Combine PLR/FT-Transformer numeric embeddings with low-rank cross layers on a dense 70-feature target
Field-count scaling curve (10->120 fields) for full vs low-rank vs DCN-Mix to calibrate the low-rank-mandatory threshold

new_direction: tabular-foundation-models: open-vocabulary graph tokenizer that LM-embeds column names to transfer across unmatched schemas
query: CARTE pretraining transfer tabular learning arxiv 2402.16785
why_new: First record covering language-model-on-column-names tokenization + graphlet row representation + contrastive YAGO pretraining; complements TabPFN-v2 (synthetic-SCM prior, per-cell) and entity-embeddings (per-category lookup) as a distinct high-cardinality/transfer paradigm.
next_followups: XTab (federated cross-table featurizer)
KEN knowledge-graph entity embeddings
skrub TableVectorizer / Gamma-Poisson high-cardinality encoder
Test CARTE tokenizer at 70+ features and with PLR numerical embeddings

new_direction: discretization-vq: RQ-VAE residual quantization of a content embedding into a short hierarchical discrete-code tuple (semantic IDs)
query: Recommender Systems with Generative Retrieval TIGER RQ-VAE semantic IDs arXiv 2305.05065
why_new: First coverage of the discretization-vq route: transferable kernel = bounded shared codebook for high-cardinality + content-derived cold-start (RQ-VAE 3+1 hierarchical codes). Recsys generative-retrieval machinery is out of scope per the topic anti-pattern; only the quantizer transfers.
next_followups: Strip RQ-VAE from recsys seq2seq; quantize a per-row or per-field-group tabular embedding
Codebook-collapse mitigation (k-means init) for high-cardinality columns
Compare RQ-VAE codes vs entity-embedding tables vs hashing for OOV/cold-start
Industrial-scale successors (RPG, LIGER, EAGER)

new_direction: temporal-feature-tokenization: variate-as-token (inverted) embedding with joint cross-feature attention
query: iTransformer inverted variate-as-token time series forecasting arXiv 2310.06625
why_new: Third anchor for the temporal route: variate-as-token = FT-Transformer feature-as-token applied to time-varying features; joint attention models cross-feature interaction (opposite of PatchTST channel-independence). Wins on high-N datasets (Traffic 862, ECL, PEMS), ties PatchTST on small ETT.
next_followups: Pair joint-variate attention with PatchTST patch tokens or PLR per-value embeddings
Test variate subsampling at 70+ mixed features
Add a categorical/static token pathway to the inverted scheme
Bridge to tabular-transformers (FT-Transformer feature-as-token)

new_direction: llm-tabular-serialization: row-to-text serialization + LLM subword tokenizer (TabLLM) — first anchor on the route
query: TabLLM few-shot tabular classification LLM serialization (arXiv:2210.10723)
why_new: First coverage of llm-tabular-serialization: manual Text Template + T0 11B + T-Few PEFT. No learned tabular tokenizer — row becomes a NL sentence tokenized by the LLM. Strong very-few-shot, but 1024-token ceiling caps it at <=30 rich columns; 106-feature case needed lossy truncation. Strongest baseline is TabPFN, not a tree. Numerics = raw digit strings (weak).
next_followups: LIFT / GReaT serialization paradigms
reconcile TabLLM vs LIFT/Dinh-2022 on column-name importance
add Gorishniy-2022 numeric embedding to serialized numerics
use permuted-names/only-values ablation as a general tokenizer test

new_direction: tabular-foundation-models: shared Set-Transformer column embedder + column-collapse-to-row-token ICL (TabICL v1)
query: TabICL in-context tabular foundation model large data (arXiv:2502.05564)
source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.05564 (PDF deep-read, ICML 2025 / PMLR 267; saved to sources/papers/)
why_new: Column-then-row attention with a SHARED Set-Transformer column embedder (ISAB hypernetwork, zero per-feature params); column-collapse to one per-row token makes the sample-axis ICL O(n^2) paid once instead of O(n^2 m). Tree-SCM-augmented synthetic prior + size curriculum. Scales to ~500K samples / 500 features; surpasses TabPFNv2+CatBoost on >10K-sample sets. Sibling/successor to tabpfn-v2.
next_followups: lift the shared column embedder into a supervised wide-table tokenizer
probe wide-table (>100 feat) extrapolation behavior
compare RoPE-feature-axis vs repeated-feature-grouping for representation collapse

new_direction: tabular-foundation-models: target-aware embedding + column-then-row attention + repeated-feature-grouping (TabICLv2, 2026 open SOTA)
query: TabICLv2 better faster scalable open tabular foundation model (arXiv:2602.11139)
why_new: Newest open tabular foundation model (Feb 2026); supersedes TabICL v1. Introduces target-aware embedding (TAE: add EmbedTAE(y) into context-row feature embeddings), column-then-row attention, repeated-feature-grouping (3 cols/token), QASSMax. O(n^2 + n*m^2); 1M samples / 500 features in ~450s; surpasses tuned RealTabPFN-2.5 on TabArena/TALENT without tuning, 10x faster than TabPFN-2.5.
next_followups: compare TAE vs target/CatBoost encoding in a supervised FT-Transformer over 70+ features
probe the n*m^2 ceiling 100->1000 features
deep-read RealTabPFN-2.5 and TabPFN-2.5 as the dethroned baselines

new_direction: tabular-transformers (deepen): intersample/row attention + contrastive CutMix/mixup pretraining (SAINT) — past the FT-Transformer scaffold
query: SAINT row attention contrastive pretraining tabular (arXiv:2106.01342)
why_new: Intersample/row attention as a learned soft-kNN over the batch; per-feature single-ReLU continuous embedding + CutMix/mixup contrastive pretraining are transferable. Quadratic (n*d)^2 projection cost is the scaling wall; GBDT-win claim is benchmark-optimistic.
next_followups: wire SAINT's per-feature ReLU embedding + contrastive pretraining onto an FT-Transformer/PLR scaffold
stress-test intersample attention at 70+ features where (n*d)^2 cost bites
check batch-composition dependence of row-attention predictions at inference

new_direction: categorical-high-cardinality (new mechanism): vocabulary-free stateless MinHash n-gram encoder + Gamma-Poisson factorization (Cerda & Varoquaux)
query: Encoding High-Cardinality String Categorical Variables Cerda Varoquaux MinHash Gamma-Poisson dirty_cat skrub (arXiv:1907.01860)
why_new: First vocabulary-free / stateless high-cardinality string encoder in the corpus; complements entity-embeddings (closed-vocab) and target-encoding (label-leaking) on the categorical-high-cardinality route. Productionized in skrub/dirty_cat, 17+ real datasets.
next_followups: MinHash-into-transformer projection probe
lexical vs semantic similarity for dirty categoricals
skrub TableVectorizer end-to-end on a 70+-feature mixed set

new_direction: tabular-transformers (challenge/yardstick): TabArena living benchmark as a fixed-budget evaluation harness
query: TabArena living benchmark machine learning tabular data (arXiv:2506.16791)
why_new: Benchmark (not a tokenizer). 51 curated IID datasets (10-1000 features, 500-250k samples), 16 models incl. 3 foundation models (TabPFNv2/TabICL/TabDPT). Foundation-model wins bounded to <=500-feature subsets; temporal data excluded by design — a yardstick for our own tokenizer and a check on foundation-model scaling claims.
next_followups: use TabArena as the fixed-budget yardstick for a from-scratch wide-table tokenizer
probe the <=500-feature ceiling where foundation-model wins are reported
note temporal exclusion — TabArena cannot validate temporal-static fusion claims

query: TabNet: Attentive Interpretable Tabular Learning (arXiv:1908.07442)
why_new: Canonical 2019 feature-selection tabular DL anchor named in topic.yaml feature-interaction-selection / tabular-transformers anchor examples; deep-read filled all required record_fields. Adds the SELECTION half (O(D) instance-wise sparsemax gate) of the interaction/selection route.
next_followups: TabNet vs FT-Transformer/SAINT controlled benchmark deltas
sparsemax-selection front-end + rich per-feature tokenizer hybrid
masked-feature SSL objective transfer to other tokenizers

new_direction: Graph-organized feature interaction as an alternative to dense attention in tabular transformers
query: T2G-Former Organizing Tabular Features into Relation Graphs (arXiv:2211.16887)
source: arXiv PDF + pypdf text extraction (deep-read; sources/papers/)
why_new: First record on a learned-sparse feature-interaction graph (FR-Graph/Graph Estimator) sitting on top of an FT-Transformer tokenizer; complements numerical-embedding and dense-attention (AutoInt/DCN-V2) records. Decouples the per-feature tokenizer from the interaction mechanism.
next_followups: Read DANets (grouped interaction baseline T2G beats)
Stress-test Graph Estimator scaling beyond 93 features
Check whether entmax-only GE removes the static-topology-freeze training hack

new_direction: Generation-side LLM serialization of tables (order-invariant arbitrary conditioning)
query: GReaT Language Models are Realistic Tabular Data Generators (ICLR 2023, arXiv:2210.06280)
why_new: Generation-side anchor of the LLM-serialization route. Key transferable nugget = random feature-order permutation -> order-invariant arbitrary-conditioning joint model. Max 47 features in eval (no 70+ evidence); raw-digit numerics; serial sampling cost.
next_followups: Apply permutation training to a non-LLM per-feature-token model
Bolt PLR numeric embeddings onto serialized numerics
Contrast LIFT (no-template fine-tune) as third serialization paradigm

new_direction: industrial wide-feature + temporal-drift benchmark for validating tokenizers in the 70+-feature setting
query: TabReD: Analyzing Pitfalls and Filling the Gaps in Tabular Deep Learning Benchmarks
why_new: Only public benchmark with both median-261 (up to 1026) features AND time-based splits — exactly this topic's regime; gives a fair verdict on which tokenizers survive wide+drifting data.
next_followups: isolate retrieval vs missing-embeddings in TabR on TabReD
feature-selection/decorrelation front-end for wide tables
use TabReD time-splits as standard tokenizer-validation harness
categorical/high-cardinality stress test not covered by TabReD

new_direction: cross-table pretraining: does a shared tabular transformer backbone transfer across heterogeneous tables
query: XTab: Cross-table Pretraining for Tabular Transformers
why_new: ICML 2023 cross-table pretraining; transfers only the shared transformer backbone (feature-interaction prior), NOT the tokenizer/featurizer (re-initialized per table). Plain FT-Transformer affine numeric tokenizer (no PLR), categorical lookup, no temporal/string. Beats no-pretrain FTT in all regimes but still loses to CatBoost; gains largest under tight finetuning budget. Federated (FedAvg) pretraining scales across many tables. 16GB memory wall dropped 20/104 datasets.
next_followups: test XTab backbone warm-start on a 70+-feature wide table with PLR numeric tokenizer swapped in
compare XTab (synthetic-free, schema-free backbone transfer) vs CARTE/TabPFN-v2 transferable tokenization
measure catastrophic forgetting vs backbone size for heavy finetuning

new_direction: foundational LLM text-serialization (classification + regression) as a cautionary anchor for the 70+-feature regime
query: LIFT: Language-Interfaced Fine-Tuning for Non-Language Machine Learning Tasks
why_new: NeurIPS 2022 serialization anchor (sibling to TabLLM/GReaT), covers classification AND regression. Cautionary for 70+-feature topic: 'low-dimensional' scope, regression collapse at p>=50, context/memory wall, raw-digit numerics, no temporal/missing handling, no deep-tabular baselines. Reusable: shuffled-names ablation protocol, two-stage pretext warm-up, data augmentation.
next_followups: import shuffled-names ablation as a general name-aware-tokenizer validation test
bolt PLR numeric embeddings onto serialized numerics instead of raw digit strings
reconcile LIFT vs TabLLM on feature-name importance (model/setting dependent)

new_direction: Soft/learned discretization for numerical features (discretization-vq route)
query: AutoDis An Embedding Learning Framework for Numerical Features in CTR Prediction arXiv 2012.08986
why_new: First end-to-end soft-discretization soft-codebook numerical tokenizer on the discretization-vq route; names SBD/DBS failure modes of hard binning; constant per-field param budget.
next_followups: Benchmark AutoDis soft-codebook vs PLE/PLR under FT-Transformer on a 70+-feature TabReD/TabArena task (AUC + params/latency)
Anneal temperature tau->0 to recover discrete codes and compare against RQ-VAE semantic IDs (tiger-rqvae)
Test adaptive-temperature network on highly heterogeneous many-feature sets

new_direction: deepen tabular-transformers anchor (foundational categorical contextualizer)
query: TabTransformer contextual embeddings arXiv:2012.06678
why_new: Foundational tabular-transformer not yet recorded; pairs with FT-Transformer/SAINT to map the categorical-tokenization lineage and the numerical-bypass limitation.
next_followups: Compare TabTransformer vs FT-Transformer numerical tokenizer head-to-head on numerical-heavy tables
Test concatenated per-column identifier at 70+ columns
Check whether generator-free per-column RTD works on binned numerical features

new_direction: MLP-based parameter-efficient ensembling as a scalable backbone for many-feature tabular DL
query: TabM: Advancing Tabular Deep Learning with Parameter-Efficient Ensembling (arXiv:2410.24210)
why_new: Adds the current #1 tabular-DL benchmark model (ICLR 2025) and reframes the route as 'linear-cost MLP ensembling beats attention tokenizers'; establishes that good per-feature PLE embeddings + a scalable backbone, not a token-sequence Transformer, are what matters for wide tables.
next_followups: BatchEnsemble (Wen et al. 2020) as the underlying technique node
Packed-Ensemble (Laurent et al. 2023)
Stack a high-cardinality + temporal tokenizer on a TabM backbone and ablate ensembling vs tokenizer gains

new_direction: tabular-transformers: ExcelFormer (semi-permeable attention + importance-weighted Mixup)
query: ExcelFormer A Neural Network Surpassing GBDTs on Tabular Data arXiv:2301.02819
why_new: First FT-Transformer-descendant in the corpus whose contribution is a noise-robustness inductive bias (importance-masked attention) + tabular Mixup variants rather than a new tokenizer; KDD'24 SOTA 'no-tuning' DNN beating tuned GBDTs
next_followups: Verify SPA/Feat-Mix transfer to 70+-feature high-cardinality tables

new_direction: feature-interaction-selection: BatchEnsemble (rank-1 adapter ensembling, origin of TabM's mechanism)
query: BatchEnsemble efficient ensemble lifelong learning rank-one fast weights arXiv 2002.06715

new_direction: tabular-transformers (challenge): TabR retrieval-augmented tabular DL — instance-axis attention as a feature-count-invariant alternative to per-feature tokenization
query: TabR retrieval-augmented tabular deep learning nearest neighbors arXiv:2307.14338
why_new: NOT a per-feature tokenizer — attention is over training instances so cost is feature-count-invariant; an alternative axis to per-feature tokenization for wide tables; beats GBDT on Grinsztajn but authors caveat retrieval is not universally beneficial; surfaces a train/deploy retrieval-leakage pitfall acute for temporal+static mixed data
next_followups: Re-add PLR numerical embeddings to isolate retrieval vs tokenizer credit

new_direction: efficient single-forward-pass ensembling as robustness wrapper (ancestor of TabM)

why_new: TabM (in corpus) descends from MIMO; deep-reading the origin paper grounds the tabular ensembling lineage and the rho capacity insight
next_followups: TabM tabular ablations vs MIMO recipe

new_direction: cell-as-token universal tabular encoder unifying numeric/categorical/text/missing

next_followups: swap UniTabE textualized numerics for PLR/periodic magnitude-aware embedding

new_direction: prompt-learning importance gate as O(P x C) replacement for O(C^2) feature self-attention

next_followups: pair Trompt importance gate with PLR/periodic numerical embeddings

new_direction: llm-tabular-serialization: magnitude-aware numerical tokenization + intra-feature name-value fusion
query: TP-BERTa Making Pre-trained Language Models Great on Tabular Prediction arXiv 2403.01841
why_new: First LM-based tabular model to give the LM a magnitude-aware numerical channel (RMT) instead of raw-string or value-times-name embeddings, plus IFA that keeps sequence length = feature count; complements GReaT/TabLLM (text serialization) and FT-Transformer (value-times-name) records already in the topic.
next_followups: CT-BERT/TabPertNet database (the pretraining source)
TapTap value-string numerics

new_direction: explicit arithmetic (additive+multiplicative) feature interaction + prompt-token linear-attention for wide tabular transformers
query: AMFormer Arithmetic Feature Interaction Is Necessary for Deep Tabular Learning arXiv 2402.02334
why_new: First record covering an explicit multiplicative-attention operator (log-space attention + exp) for tabular DL and the prompt-token query trick that makes attention linear in feature count; complements additive-only interaction routes (AutoInt, DCN-V2) and the FT-Transformer tokenizer baseline.
next_followups: Neural Arithmetic Logic Units (Trask 2018) as the multiplicative-operator origin
TANGOS gradient-orthogonalization regularizer cited as sparsity confirmation
Enouen & Liu 2022 Sparse Interaction Additive Networks (synthetic-data inspiration and sparse-interaction prior)

new_direction: temporal-feature-tokenization: role-asymmetric patch+variate tokenization with a global bridge token + 1xC exogenous cross-attention
query: TimeXer Empowering Transformers for Time Series Forecasting with Exogenous Variables arXiv 2402.19072
why_new: First record of role-asymmetric tokenization (fine patch tokens for the target series, one coarse variate token per context feature) with a learnable global bridge token funneling many context features into a target via linear-in-C cross-attention; complements PatchTST (patching) and iTransformer (variate-as-token) and gives a concrete linear-in-feature-count lever for the 70+ setting.
next_followups: add a categorical/static token pathway to the role-asymmetric scheme for genuine mixed-type fusion
stress-test the single-global-token bottleneck vs multi-global-token at 70+ interacting features

next_followups: TimesFM
Moirai
MOMENT

new_direction: industrial-feature-systems: production CTR feature encoding (DLRM)

new_direction: timeseries-foundation-models: universal forecasting transformer with any-variate attention (Moirai)

Key transfer: parameter-free field-provenance encoding for 70+ features; key break: quadratic in (variates x length), no categorical/static path.

new_direction: Pre-tuned MLP + tuned-default GBDTs as a no-HPO strong baseline / linear-cost backbone for wide tabular feature sets
query: RealMLP Better by Default Strong Pre-Tuned MLPs Boosted Trees tabular arxiv 2407.04491
why_new: First record covering PBLD periodic numerical embeddings, a learnable diagonal per-feature scaling layer, and the meta-tuned-default (TD) methodology.
next_followups: Compare PBLD vs PLR/PLE on a native 70+-feature table
Stress-test fixed-8-dim high-cardinality embedding
Probe diagonal scaling layer as feature-selection front-end

new_direction: high-cardinality categorical encoding practice in winning Kaggle/production tabular pipelines (target/out-of-fold, count, CatBoost ordered TS)
query: Kaggle winning solution target encoding out-of-fold high cardinality leave-one-out smoothing count encoding; CatBoost ordered target statistics arXiv:1706.09516; Micci-Barreca 2001; Pargent et al. arXiv:2104.00629
source: Kaggle writeups + CatBoost (NeurIPS 2018, PDF saved sources/papers/catboost-1706.09516.pdf) + Micci-Barreca 2001 + Pargent et al. arXiv:2104.00629
why_new: Synthesized practitioner writeup grounded in 3 canonical sources + libraries (category_encoders/sklearn TargetEncoder/CatBoost). Distinct from existing encoding-high-cardinality-string-categoricals (vocabulary-free string n-gram codes) — this work = supervised target/count/ordered scalar encoding.
next_followups: target-encoded scalar into an attention/MLP tokenizer (does the GBDT win transfer?)
time-respecting OOF folds vs CatBoost ordered TS on transaction data
GLMM/mixed-model target encoding vs empirical-Bayes smoothing

new_direction: deepen timeseries-foundation-models route with continuous patch tokenizer (TimesFM)
query: TimesFM decoder-only foundation model time-series forecasting arxiv 2310.10688
why_new: Adds the continuous patch-token + MSE decoder-only foundation model as the foil to Chronos value-binning and PatchTST encoder patching for the time-varying half of the feature set
next_followups: TimesFM-2.x covariate-aware extension
compare continuous patch tokens vs Chronos bins under a shared multi-feature fusion layer

new_direction: deepen libraries-and-implementations with the rtdl reference tokenizer hub (FT-Transformer + PLE/Periodic/PLR)
query: rtdl yandex-research tabular deep learning reference library FT-Transformer numerical embeddings PLR
why_new: Reference implementation hub for FT-Transformer (rtdl_revisiting_models, Apache-2.0) and PLE/Periodic/PLR numerical embeddings (rtdl_num_embeddings, MIT v0.0.12). Old monolithic rtdl deprecated. Linformer kv-compression is the only native scaling lever; no in-library 70+-feature benchmark; numerical+categorical only.
next_followups: benchmark PLR/PLE rtdl modules + Linformer kv-compression at 70+ features under a time-based split
wire rtdl_num_embeddings into pytorch-frame stype registry for a runnable heterogeneous tokenizer

new_direction: deepen tabular-foundation-models with current SOTA TabPFN-2.5 (feature-cell grouping pushes the validated feature ceiling to 2,000)
query: TabPFN-2.5 scaling tabular foundation model larger datasets feature counts
source: arxiv.org/abs/2511.08667 (dispatch URL 2509.16078 was WRONG; corrected via web search)
why_new: Direct successor to TabPFN v2; validates per-cell dual-attention tokenizer at 50k x 2k (4x feature ceiling) via feature-cell grouping (group size 3) — first tabular foundation model comfortably covering the topic's 70+-feature target on small/medium tables.
next_followups: TabPFN-Wide arXiv:2510.06162 (extreme feature counts via continued pretraining)
TabPFN-TS temporal extension
LimiX head-to-head wide-table comparison
open distill-to-MLP reproduction of the proprietary deployment path

new_direction: continued pre-training + synthetic feature-widening prior to extend a tabular foundation model to extreme feature counts (HDLSS)
query: TabPFN-Wide continued pre-training extreme feature counts arXiv 2510.06162
why_new: first work in the memory showing the feature-count ceiling of a per-feature tabular tokenizer (TabPFNv2, ~500 feats) can be pushed to 30k-70k purely by prior-engineering + continued pre-training, with NO tokenizer/architecture change — a data-side rather than architecture-side scaling lever; also surfaces a design tension with TabPFN-2.5's feature-grouping approach
next_followups: Real-TabPFN (Garg et al. 2025) continued-pretraining-on-real-data sibling
TabFlex linear-attention scaling (Zeng et al. 2025)
TuneTables context optimization (Feuer et al. 2024)
Ye et al. 2025 'A closer look at TabPFN v2' for attention-evolution-across-layers

new_direction: temporal-static-fusion via time-conditioned feature modulation
query: Feature-aware Modulation for Learning from Temporal Tabular Data arXiv:2512.03678
why_new: First record for FiLM-style time-conditioned per-feature (Yeo-Johnson + affine) modulation on TabReD; covers temporal-static-fusion + numerical-embeddings layers and flags PLR-embedding conflict.
next_followups: read Cai & Ye 2025 (TabReD temporal embedding origin, ref [6])
read Rubachev TabReD benchmark (ref [41]) for feature counts/cardinality per dataset
read Drift-Resilient TabPFN (ref [15]) as adaptive alternative

new_direction: temporal-feature-tokenization: TabFormer (TabBERT/TabGPT) hierarchical tokenization of multivariate tabular time series
query: Tabular Transformers for Modeling Multivariate Time Series TabBERT TabFormer arXiv 2011.01843
why_new: First transformer treatment of tabular TIME SERIES; canonical IBM credit-card fraud benchmark; supplies the per-field-quantization + field->sequence hierarchy that is the temporal-static-fusion skeleton for the 70+-mixed-feature goal — not previously in the index.
next_followups: Cross-link to PatchTST/iTransformer/TFT/TimeXer for temporal tokenizer comparison
Pair with On-Embeddings-Numerical-Features / AutoDis to fix the weak quantization encoder
Look for successors (UniTabE, TP-BERTa) that scale the hierarchy to many fields

next_followups: TabPFNv2 / TabICL deep-read

why_new: RQ-Kmeans is the k-means alternative to TIGER's RQ-VAE codebooks; fixes codebook collapse with training-free clustering on residuals; not previously recorded. Task-supplied URL 2406.13235 was wrong (GAL-Rec) and was corrected to the OneRec sources.

new_direction: tabular-foundation-models: per-cell DFE + masked joint-distribution open-weights foundation model
query: LimiX large tabular foundation model arXiv 2509.03505 discriminative feature encoding CCMM
why_new: Apache-2.0 open-weights TabPFN-v2/TabICL-lineage in-context model; per-cell tokens + DFE low-rank column-identity code + CCMM masked joint-distribution over synthetic SCM/DAG priors; top-ranked across 11 benchmarks. Dispatch title 'vs TabPFN-2.5/TabICLv2' is anachronistic (benchmarks TabPFN-v2/TabICL v1) — flagged as pitfall.
